authoritative_sources.title: Constituent Services: Overview and Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CRS is Congress's nonpartisan shared research staff, and this report directly defines constituent-services casework, typical request types, agency interaction limits, and the administrative-support posture of congressional offices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: casework intake, agency liaison packaging, constituent communication, and unsupported-pressure boundary setting

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-11, Section 280: Managing Customer Experience and Improving Service Delivery
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB issues executive-branch budget and management policy, and Section 280 is the federal baseline for customer-experience and service-delivery governance, including service standards, customer feedback, high-impact services, and journey accountability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service intake, support resolution, customer communication, complaint tracking, and tenant service-model adaptation

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552: Public information; agency rules, opinions, orders, records, and proceedings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official U.S. Code text for FOIA governs federal agency public-record access and creates the records-release boundary for constituent services when a request involves agency records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public-records routing, disclosure boundary setting, and transparency-log handling

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552a: Records maintained on individuals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official Privacy Act text governs federal records maintained on individuals and is authoritative for consent, disclosure, amendment, and protected-record boundaries in constituent casework.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy release validation, identity-sensitive casework, protected-record handling, and disclosure escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Agency FOIA Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Office of Information Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ OIP is responsible for encouraging agency FOIA compliance, and its guidance is authoritative for FOIA intake, requester communication, fee, identity, routing, and customer-service handling in agency public-record workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FOIA intake triage, requester-contact summary, public-records handoff, and unresolved-request packaging

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 1.602-2: Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council via Acquisition.GOV
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FAR is the primary federal procurement regulation, and FAR 1.602-2 defines contracting officer and COR responsibility boundaries that constituent-services work must respect when a vendor inquiry or protest communication enters the lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: procurement-sensitive inquiry triage, solicitation handoff, vendor communication boundary setting, and contracting-authority refusal

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 555: Ancillary matters
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official Administrative Procedure Act provision supplies due-process guardrails for agency matters, including representation, prompt notice, and written denial statements, which define when casework must defer to official process.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rights-sensitive notice handling, appeal or denial escalation, and due-process boundary setting

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized internal-control standard for federal entities and is authoritative for documentation, control activities, monitoring, and audit evidence expectations that keep public-service support fair and traceable.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audit-trail construction, evidence capture, control-aware escalation, and readiness evidence review

workflow_stages.name: Constituent intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requester role, jurisdiction, program, agency, channel, case identifiers, privacy release or consent, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Official-record and prerequisite review
workflow_stages.description: Validate case files, applications, notices, correspondence logs, permit records, public-record request IDs, procurement references, and audit artifacts so the response is tied to official records rather than constituent assertions alone.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Supported resolution and public-service communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare resolution notes and stakeholder communication summaries that explain documented status, action taken, missing items, and safe next steps without promising adjudicative, legal, procurement, or records-release outcomes.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility, rights, notice, and due-process escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate adverse notices, appeal deadlines, eligibility disputes, statutory-rights questions, legal interpretation, or missing due-process records to the responsible adjudication or legal owner.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Records, privacy, procurement, and enforcement boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Route FOIA, Privacy Act, protected-record, procurement, vendor, enforcement, inspection, and complaint matters when the requested action belongs to public records, privacy, procurement, enforcement, audit, or legal owners.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Handoff, closure, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm resolution versus handoff, record the next owner, preserve official-record traceability, and apply tenant-specific service models only when jurisdiction, policy, authority, and records dependencies are explicit.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

artifacts: constituent intake records, casework requests, contact history, correspondence logs, and agency liaison notes
privacy releases, consent forms, representative authorizations, identity verification records, and disclosure caveats
applications, case files, eligibility records, benefit notices, adverse action notices, appeal windows, hearing references, and program-status records
permit applications, inspection records, permit decisions, complaint records, enforcement referrals, and service-ticket history
FOIA or public-records requests, Privacy Act requests, records-location notes, transparency logs, and disclosure or exemption routing notes
procurement documents, solicitation numbers, vendor communications, contracting officer or COR references, protest-sensitive artifacts, and audit trails
resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, escalation packages, request-resolution summaries, and exception communication escalation notes
systems_of_record: constituent relationship management or correspondence management system
benefits, permits, licensing, or public-service administration system
public records or FOIA tracking system
complaint, inspection, or enforcement referral system
knowledge base containing tenant policy, statutes, agency manuals, service standards, and appeal rules

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, informational, exception-driven, complaint-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, jurisdiction, official records, privacy permissions, and authoritative source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, service-rule summaries, missing-item lists, and stakeholder communication that stay within documented authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when eligibility, due process, public records, procurement, legal, enforcement, audit, privacy, compensation, or policy ownership shifts.
escalate: Required case, notice, application, permit, public-records, procurement, privacy, or correspondence evidence is missing or materially conflicts.

The request requires eligibility determination, adverse-action reconsideration, appeal handling, legal interpretation, FOIA release, Privacy Act disclosure, procurement authority, enforcement action, compensation approval, or safety-critical decisioning.
A statute, agency manual, tenant policy, due-process notice, or system-of-record status materially conflicts with another authoritative source.
The case involves repeat failure, public harm, rights impact, equity concern, or audit-trail gap that exceeds documented constituent-services authority.
refuse: Promise a benefit, permit, appeal, enforcement, procurement, compensation, refund, records-release, or legal outcome that the authoritative record does not support.
Override public eligibility, appeal, procurement, records, privacy, or policy limits without explicit authority.
Impersonate official adjudication, enforcement, legal, contracting, public-records, privacy, audit, or financial-approval authority.

domain_failure_modes: the lane pressures an agency or promises a public-service outcome instead of preserving administrative casework boundaries
a benefits, permit, or eligibility case is resolved without the required official record, notice, privacy release, or audit trail
a FOIA, Privacy Act, or protected-record question is answered directly rather than routed to public records, privacy, or legal owners
a procurement-sensitive vendor complaint is handled as ordinary constituent service instead of being handed to procurement or legal
an adverse notice or appeal deadline is summarized without preserving due-process evidence and escalation
repeat agency service failures are treated as routine status checks instead of complaint escalation

the government-and-public-sector label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic customer support language without constituent, records, eligibility, due-process, procurement, or audit terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, program manuals, state or local public-records law, tenant privacy procedures, and office casework protocols can materially narrow the federal public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package uses federal and official public-sector sources as a validated baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, agency manuals, program rules, appeal windows, public-records procedures, privacy-release requirements, and procurement authority before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Constituent-services practices differ between elected offices, agency service centers, ombuds offices, and local government contact centers.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane is constrained to administrative service support, communication, evidence packaging, and escalation. Tenant configuration must identify the operating model and authority matrix before production routing.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, records handling, due process, procurement, public-records, privacy, and failure behavior instead of only generic customer-service scripting.

The lane stops at constituent service support and communication. Program adjudication, enforcement, legal interpretation, public-records release, procurement authority, audit signoff, and financial approval remain outside the lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because tenant-specific conflict points are handled as explicit retrieval and escalation dependencies.
